name: OAK-D Lite Stereo Depth Camera
description: Stereo depth camera with onboard AI inference for RGB vision, object detection, and depth mapping. Ideal for robots that need to see, identify objects, and understand spatial layout.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: oak-d-lite-vision
hardware: OAK-D Lite Stereo Depth Camera
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, ROS2 Humble, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to front_chin bracket using M3 screws, connect USB-C to onboard computer
  software:
    - pip install depthai opencv-python
    - ros2 launch depthai_ros_driver camera.launch.py
agent_tools:
  - name: get_camera_image
    description: Returns RGB image as numpy array (640x480)
    entrypoint: skills/vision/get_camera_image.py
  - name: detect_objects
    description: Returns list of detected objects with bounding boxes and labels using onboard neural inference
    entrypoint: skills/vision/detect_objects.py
  - name: get_stereo_depth
    description: Returns stereo depth map in millimeters (640x480)
    entrypoint: skills/vision/get_stereo_depth.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have stereo vision with onboard AI inference. Call get_camera_image() for RGB frames, detect_objects() for neural object detection, and get_stereo_depth() for depth maps.

name: Intel RealSense D435i Depth Camera
description: High-precision depth camera with built-in IMU for measuring distances, generating 3D point clouds, and obstacle detection up to 10 meters. Best for navigation and spatial mapping tasks.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: depth-perception
hardware: Intel RealSense D435i Depth Camera
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount camera to front_chin mount point using M3 screws, route USB-C cable through cable management to onboard computer
  software:
    - pip install pyrealsense2
    - pip install open3d
    - ros2 launch realsense2_camera rs_launch.py
agent_tools:
  - name: get_depth_image
    description: Returns depth map as numpy array with distance values in meters
    entrypoint: skills/depth_perception/get_depth_image.py
  - name: detect_obstacles
    description: Returns list of obstacle positions and distances within specified range
    entrypoint: skills/depth_perception/detect_obstacles.py
  - name: estimate_object_distance
    description: Returns distance in meters to object at specified pixel coordinates
    entrypoint: skills/depth_perception/estimate_object_distance.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have depth perception through the Intel RealSense D435i. Measure distances to objects up to 10m away and generate 3D point clouds.

name: Slamtec RPLIDAR A1 - 360 Laser Range Scanner
description: 360-degree laser scanner for SLAM-based mapping and autonomous navigation. Enables the robot to build maps of its environment and localize itself within them.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: rplidar-a1-localization
hardware: Slamtec RPLIDAR A1 - 360 Laser Range Scanner
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, ROS2 Humble, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to back_plate using M3 screws, ensure 360-degree clearance for rotating head, connect USB adapter cable
  software:
    - pip install rplidar-roboticia
    - ros2 launch rplidar_ros rplidar_a1_launch.py
agent_tools:
  - name: scan_360
    description: Returns 360-degree distance measurements as list of (angle, distance_mm) tuples
    entrypoint: skills/localization/scan_360.py
  - name: localize_slam
    description: Returns estimated (x, y, theta) pose using SLAM with the current map
    entrypoint: skills/localization/localize_slam.py
  - name: navigate_to_point
    description: Plans and executes obstacle-avoiding path to target (x, y) coordinates
    entrypoint: skills/localization/navigate_to_point.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have 360-degree LiDAR scanning. Use scan_360() for raw distance data, localize_slam() for position estimation, and navigate_to_point() for autonomous navigation.

name: Garmin LIDAR-Lite Optical Distance Sensor V3
description: Precision optical rangefinder for long-range distance measurement up to 40 meters. Useful for obstacle avoidance, terrain following, and proximity detection at range.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: garmin-lidar-distance
hardware: Garmin LIDAR-Lite Optical Distance Sensor V3
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to front_chin using M2 screws, connect I2C (SDA/SCL) to GPIO breakout
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-lidarlite smbus2
agent_tools:
  - name: get_distance
    description: Returns distance to nearest object in centimeters (0-4000cm range)
    entrypoint: skills/distance_sensing/get_distance.py
  - name: continuous_distance_stream
    description: Returns async generator of distance readings at 500Hz
    entrypoint: skills/distance_sensing/continuous_distance_stream.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have a precision distance sensor. Use get_distance() for single measurements or continuous_distance_stream() for real-time ranging up to 40 meters.

name: Robotiq 2F-85 Adaptive Gripper
description: Industrial-grade adaptive gripper with force feedback for precise pick-and-place operations. Can grasp objects of varying shapes and sizes with automatic grip strength adjustment.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: robotiq-2f85-gripper
hardware: Robotiq 2F-85 Gripper
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, ROS2 Humble, Python 3.10]
installation:
  physical: Mount gripper to front_chin using M6 bolts. Connect RS485-USB interface to onboard computer USB port. Route power cable to 12V supply rail.
  software:
    - pip install robotiq-2f-gripper-control pymodbus
    - ros2 launch robotiq_2f_gripper_control robotiq_gripper.launch.py port:=/dev/ttyUSB0
agent_tools:
  - name: grip_object
    description: Closes gripper fingers to specified position (0-255) and returns grasp success status
    entrypoint: skills/manipulation/grip_object.py
  - name: release_object
    description: Opens gripper fingers completely and returns release confirmation
    entrypoint: skills/manipulation/release_object.py
  - name: get_gripper_status
    description: Returns current finger position, force feedback, and object detection status
    entrypoint: skills/manipulation/get_gripper_status.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have a Robotiq 2F-85 gripper for precise manipulation. The gripper detects object presence through force feedback and adjusts grip strength automatically.

name: Micro Servo Gripper Kit
description: Lightweight budget gripper for basic pick-and-place tasks with objects under 200g. Simple servo-driven open/close mechanism, ideal for light manipulation needs.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: micro-servo-gripper
hardware: Micro Servo Gripper Kit
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount gripper assembly to front_chin rail using included bracket, connect servo to PWM-capable USB servo controller
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-servokit
agent_tools:
  - name: grip_object
    description: Closes servo gripper to specified angle (0-180) and returns position
    entrypoint: skills/manipulation/grip_object.py
  - name: release_object
    description: Opens servo gripper fully and returns confirmation
    entrypoint: skills/manipulation/release_object.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have a lightweight servo gripper for basic pick-and-place tasks. Grip strength is limited but suitable for objects under 200g.

name: Adafruit 9-DOF Absolute Orientation IMU (BNO055)
description: 9-axis absolute orientation sensor providing heading, roll, pitch, and linear acceleration. Enables the robot to know its exact orientation and detect tilt or rotation.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: bno055-orientation
hardware: Adafruit 9-DOF Absolute Orientation IMU (BNO055)
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount breakout to back_plate using M2.5 standoffs, connect I2C to GPIO header
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-bno055
agent_tools:
  - name: get_orientation
    description: Returns (heading, roll, pitch) in degrees as absolute orientation
    entrypoint: skills/orientation/get_orientation.py
  - name: get_linear_acceleration
    description: Returns (x, y, z) linear acceleration in m/s^2 without gravity
    entrypoint: skills/orientation/get_linear_acceleration.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have 9-DOF absolute orientation sensing. Use get_orientation() for heading/roll/pitch and get_linear_acceleration() for motion detection.

name: Adafruit AMG8833 IR Thermal Camera Breakout
description: 8x8 infrared thermal imaging sensor for detecting heat sources like humans, animals, and running machinery. Useful for search-and-rescue, security patrols, and leak detection.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: amg8833-thermal
hardware: Adafruit AMG8833 IR Thermal Camera Breakout
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to front_chin using M2.5 screws, connect I2C to GPIO breakout
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-amg88xx scipy
agent_tools:
  - name: get_thermal_image
    description: Returns 8x8 grid of temperature readings in Celsius
    entrypoint: skills/thermal_vision/get_thermal_image.py
  - name: detect_heat_sources
    description: Returns list of (row, col, temperature) for pixels above threshold
    entrypoint: skills/thermal_vision/detect_heat_sources.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have thermal vision via the AMG8833 sensor. Use get_thermal_image() for an 8x8 heat map and detect_heat_sources() to find warm objects like humans or running motors.

name: Adafruit Mini GPS PA1010D Module
description: Compact GPS/GNSS receiver for outdoor positioning and navigation. Provides latitude, longitude, altitude, and ground speed for waypoint-based outdoor missions.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: pa1010d-gps
hardware: Adafruit Mini GPS PA1010D Module
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to back_plate with clear sky view, connect I2C to GPIO header
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-gps pyserial
agent_tools:
  - name: get_gps_position
    description: Returns (latitude, longitude, altitude) from GPS fix
    entrypoint: skills/gps_navigation/get_gps_position.py
  - name: get_gps_speed
    description: Returns ground speed in m/s and heading in degrees
    entrypoint: skills/gps_navigation/get_gps_speed.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have GPS positioning for outdoor navigation. Use get_gps_position() for lat/lon coordinates and get_gps_speed() for velocity tracking.

name: Adafruit VL53L4CD Time of Flight Distance Sensor
description: Precision short-range laser distance sensor (1-130cm) for close-range obstacle detection, object approach, and docking maneuvers.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: vl53l4cd-tof
hardware: Adafruit VL53L4CD Time of Flight Distance Sensor
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to front_chin using M2 screws, connect I2C (SDA/SCL) to GPIO breakout
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-vl53l4cd
agent_tools:
  - name: get_distance_mm
    description: Returns distance to nearest object in millimeters (10-1300mm range)
    entrypoint: skills/distance_sensing/get_distance_mm.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have precise short-range distance sensing (1-130cm). Ideal for close-range obstacle detection and object approach.

name: HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Distance Sensor
description: Budget ultrasonic rangefinder for simple obstacle avoidance (2-400cm). Reliable, low-cost distance measurement using sound waves.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: hcsr04-ultrasonic
hardware: HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Distance Sensor
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to front_chin using bracket, connect trigger and echo pins to GPIO
  software:
    - pip install RPi.GPIO
agent_tools:
  - name: get_distance_cm
    description: Returns distance to nearest object in centimeters (2-400cm range)
    entrypoint: skills/distance_sensing/get_distance_cm.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have ultrasonic ranging (2-400cm). Simple, reliable distance measurement for obstacle avoidance.

name: Adafruit 16-Channel PWM Servo Driver (PCA9685)
description: 16-channel servo motor controller for coordinated multi-joint movements. Drives up to 16 servos simultaneously for robotic arms, pan-tilt mechanisms, and articulated joints.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: pca9685-servo-driver
hardware: Adafruit 16-Channel PWM Servo Driver (PCA9685)
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to back_plate using M2.5 standoffs, connect I2C to GPIO header, connect servo power supply
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-pca9685 adafruit-circuitpython-servokit
agent_tools:
  - name: set_servo_angle
    description: Sets specified servo channel (0-15) to target angle (0-180 degrees)
    entrypoint: skills/servo_control/set_servo_angle.py
  - name: set_all_servos
    description: Sets all connected servos to specified angles simultaneously
    entrypoint: skills/servo_control/set_all_servos.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have 16-channel servo control. Use set_servo_angle() for individual servos or set_all_servos() for coordinated multi-joint movements.

name: ReSpeaker USB Mic Array v2.0
description: Directional microphone array with beamforming for voice command recognition, sound source localization, and ambient sound classification. Enables the robot to hear and respond to spoken instructions.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: respeaker-audio
hardware: ReSpeaker USB Mic Array v2.0
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, ROS2 Humble, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount mic array to back_plate top surface for unobstructed audio capture, connect USB-A to onboard computer
  software:
    - pip install pyaudio webrtcvad speechrecognition
    - sudo apt install portaudio19-dev
agent_tools:
  - name: listen_for_command
    description: Records audio and returns transcribed speech text using voice activity detection
    entrypoint: skills/audio_perception/listen_for_command.py
  - name: get_sound_direction
    description: Returns angle in degrees (0-360) of dominant sound source using beamforming
    entrypoint: skills/audio_perception/get_sound_direction.py
  - name: detect_sound_event
    description: Classifies ambient sounds (clap, whistle, alarm, speech) and returns event type with confidence
    entrypoint: skills/audio_perception/detect_sound_event.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have directional audio perception. Use listen_for_command() for speech-to-text, get_sound_direction() to locate sound sources, and detect_sound_event() for ambient audio classification.

name: Adafruit I2S 3W Stereo Speaker Bonnet
description: Audio output module for text-to-speech communication and alert sounds. Allows the robot to speak to humans, announce status, and play warning tones.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: speaker-audio-output
hardware: Adafruit I2S 3W Stereo Speaker Bonnet
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount bonnet to back_plate, connect to I2S pins on GPIO header, attach 3W speakers to screw terminals
  software:
    - pip install pyttsx3 pygame
    - sudo apt install espeak
agent_tools:
  - name: speak_text
    description: Converts text to speech and plays through speakers using TTS engine
    entrypoint: skills/audio_output/speak_text.py
  - name: play_alert
    description: Plays predefined alert sound (warning, success, error, attention)
    entrypoint: skills/audio_output/play_alert.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have audio output capability. Use speak_text() to communicate verbally with humans and play_alert() for status notifications.

name: Google Coral USB Accelerator
description: Edge TPU hardware accelerator for running neural network inference at 30+ FPS. Enables real-time object detection, image classification, and pose estimation without cloud connectivity.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: coral-tpu-accelerator
hardware: Google Coral USB Accelerator
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount USB accelerator to back_plate using adhesive pad, connect USB-C to onboard computer
  software:
    - pip install pycoral tflite-runtime
    - sudo apt install libedgetpu1-std
agent_tools:
  - name: run_inference
    description: Runs TFLite model on Edge TPU, returns inference results with <5ms latency
    entrypoint: skills/ai_acceleration/run_inference.py
  - name: classify_image
    description: Classifies image using MobileNet on TPU, returns top-5 labels with confidence scores
    entrypoint: skills/ai_acceleration/classify_image.py
  - name: detect_objects_fast
    description: Runs SSD object detection on TPU at 30+ FPS, returns bounding boxes and labels
    entrypoint: skills/ai_acceleration/detect_objects_fast.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have hardware AI acceleration via the Coral Edge TPU. Run neural network inference at 30+ FPS for real-time object detection and classification.

name: Adafruit Feather nRF52840 Express
description: Bluetooth Low Energy module for wireless communication with nearby devices, beacons, and monitoring stations. Enables mesh networking and remote status broadcasting.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: nrf52840-wireless
hardware: Adafruit Feather nRF52840 Express
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount feather to back_plate using header pins, connect USB-C for serial bridge to onboard computer
  software:
    - pip install bleak asyncio
agent_tools:
  - name: scan_ble_devices
    description: Scans for nearby BLE devices and returns list of (name, address, rssi) tuples
    entrypoint: skills/wireless_communication/scan_ble_devices.py
  - name: send_ble_message
    description: Sends data payload to connected BLE device by address
    entrypoint: skills/wireless_communication/send_ble_message.py
  - name: broadcast_status
    description: Broadcasts robot status as BLE advertisement visible to nearby devices
    entrypoint: skills/wireless_communication/broadcast_status.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have BLE wireless communication. Scan for nearby devices, send messages to paired peripherals, and broadcast status to monitoring stations.

name: Adafruit BME680 Environmental Sensor
description: Multi-sensor for temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, and air quality (VOC gas). Useful for environmental monitoring, hazard detection, and climate-aware mission planning.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: bme680-environment
hardware: Adafruit BME680 Environmental Sensor
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount to back_plate with exposure to ambient air, connect I2C to GPIO header
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-bme680
agent_tools:
  - name: get_environment
    description: Returns temperature (C), humidity (%), pressure (hPa), and gas resistance (ohms)
    entrypoint: skills/environment_sensing/get_environment.py
  - name: get_air_quality_index
    description: Returns computed air quality index (0-500) based on VOC gas resistance
    entrypoint: skills/environment_sensing/get_air_quality_index.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have environmental sensing. Monitor temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, and air quality for situational awareness.

name: Adafruit NeoPixel Ring 24 RGB LED
description: Programmable RGB LED ring for visual status indication and expressive feedback. Shows robot state (red=error, green=ok, blue=thinking) and animated patterns visible to nearby humans.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: neopixel-status-ring
hardware: Adafruit NeoPixel Ring 24 RGB LED
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount LED ring to front_chin bezel using adhesive backing, connect data pin to GPIO and 5V power
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-neopixel rpi_ws281x
agent_tools:
  - name: set_status_color
    description: Sets all LEDs to specified RGB color for status indication (red=error, green=ok, blue=thinking)
    entrypoint: skills/status_lighting/set_status_color.py
  - name: animate_pattern
    description: Plays LED animation pattern (pulse, chase, rainbow, breathe) for visual feedback
    entrypoint: skills/status_lighting/animate_pattern.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have visual status lighting. Use set_status_color() to indicate robot state and animate_pattern() for expressive visual feedback to nearby humans.

name: Adafruit LSM6DSOX 6-DOF Accel + Gyro IMU
description: Precision 6-axis motion sensor for detecting acceleration, angular velocity, impacts, and falls. Useful for terrain assessment, collision detection, and stability monitoring.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: lsm6dsox-motion
hardware: Adafruit LSM6DSOX 6-DOF Accel + Gyro IMU
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Mount breakout to back_plate center using M2 standoffs for minimal vibration noise, connect I2C to GPIO header
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-lsm6ds
agent_tools:
  - name: get_acceleration
    description: Returns (x, y, z) acceleration in m/s^2
    entrypoint: skills/motion_detection/get_acceleration.py
  - name: get_gyro
    description: Returns (x, y, z) angular velocity in degrees/second
    entrypoint: skills/motion_detection/get_gyro.py
  - name: detect_fall
    description: Returns true if free-fall or impact event detected via hardware interrupt
    entrypoint: skills/motion_detection/detect_fall.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have precision motion detection. Monitor acceleration, angular velocity, and detect impacts or falls in real-time.

name: Adafruit INA260 High/Low Side Current + Power Sensor
description: Real-time power monitoring sensor for tracking battery current draw, voltage, and estimating remaining runtime. Essential for energy-aware mission planning on battery-powered robots.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: ina260-power-monitor
hardware: Adafruit INA260 High/Low Side Current + Power Sensor
compatibility: [Unitree A1, Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10+]
installation:
  physical: Wire inline with main battery supply on back_plate, connect I2C to GPIO header
  software:
    - pip install adafruit-circuitpython-ina260
agent_tools:
  - name: get_power_status
    description: Returns current (mA), voltage (V), and power (mW) readings from main battery bus
    entrypoint: skills/power_monitoring/get_power_status.py
  - name: estimate_battery_life
    description: Returns estimated remaining runtime in minutes based on current draw rate
    entrypoint: skills/power_monitoring/estimate_battery_life.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have power monitoring. Track real-time current draw, voltage, and estimate remaining battery life to plan energy-aware missions.

name: Unitree G1 Bipedal Locomotion Kit
description: Complete 12-DOF leg actuator system for the Unitree G1 humanoid. Includes 6 joints per leg (hip pitch/roll/yaw, knee, ankle pitch/roll), pre-trained RL walking policy, and PD controllers. Enables walking, running, turning, and dynamic balance.
skill_yaml:
skill_id: g1-bipedal-locomotion
hardware: Unitree G1 Bipedal Locomotion Kit
compatibility: [Unitree G1, MuJoCo, Ubuntu 22.04]
installation:
  physical: Integrated 12-DOF leg assembly — 6 joints per leg (3 hip + knee + 2 ankle). Torque-controlled via CAN bus.
  software:
    - pip install torch mujoco
    - Deploy RL locomotion policy (deploy/g1_policy.pt)
    - Configure PD gains: hip kp=100 kd=2, knee kp=150 kd=4, ankle kp=40 kd=2
agent_tools:
  - name: walk_forward
    description: Walk forward at specified speed (0.1-1.0 m/s) using trained RL gait policy
    entrypoint: agents/executor.py
  - name: walk_backward
    description: Walk backward at specified speed
    entrypoint: agents/executor.py
  - name: turn
    description: Turn left or right at specified yaw rate (rad/s)
    entrypoint: agents/executor.py
  - name: stop
    description: Come to a balanced standing stop
    entrypoint: agents/executor.py
agent_context_update: >
  You now have bipedal locomotion via a reinforcement-learning-trained gait policy. Use walk_forward(speed), walk_backward(speed), turn(direction, rate), and stop() to move. The RL policy handles balance, gait generation, and dynamic stability automatically.
